# Feature: Request Editor Clipboard UX

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-QS-001  
**Source**: Quick Spec (self-contained — no plan file)

Add consistent copy/paste UX to the request editors for headers, query params, raw body, and URL-encoded body while preserving the platform-agnostic core architecture. Table-based editors must copy only enabled non-empty rows, preserve duplicate keys, and paste parsed clipboard rows at the end of the existing table instead of overwriting existing data. Raw body copy/paste remains plain text, but clipboard access must be routed through the platform adapter so the same UI works in web and VS Code.

Out of scope for this feature: multipart form-data clipboard support and the broader migration of every remaining clipboard usage in `packages/core`.

## 2. Technical Context

> This section captures the key technical decisions for this feature. It replaces the plan file reference — the Implement sub-agent reads this for context instead.
> Standing rules (coding standards, architecture, testing, logging) are in `.spec-lite/memory.md` — only list additions or overrides here.

### Approach

Add a dedicated clipboard adapter to the existing platform adapter boundary instead of calling `navigator.clipboard` directly from core components. Core request-editor components will use a new clipboard hook exposed from `useAdapter.tsx`, and platform implementations will provide the environment-specific clipboard mechanism:

- Web adapter uses browser clipboard APIs internally.
- VS Code adapter forwards clipboard requests through the existing webview-to-extension bridge and uses the extension host clipboard API.

Create a shared request-clipboard utility in `packages/core` that handles both serialization and parsing for key/value table editors. The utility must:

- Serialize only enabled rows with both `key` and `value` populated.
- Output newline-delimited `key=value` text for consistent copy behavior across headers, params, and URL-encoded body.
- Parse JSON objects into key/value rows.
- Parse newline-delimited `key=value` and `key: value` text.
- Ignore a trailing comma immediately before a newline.
- Preserve duplicate keys.
- Return append-ready row values so existing table content remains intact.

Each request editor owns its own append logic so the behavior matches the store shape already used by that editor:

- `RequestHeaders.tsx` appends parsed rows to the current headers list and preserves the trailing empty row behavior already present in the UI.
- `RequestParams.tsx` appends parsed rows to the current params list and preserves the trailing empty row behavior already present in the UI.
- `FormBody.tsx` appends parsed rows to the end of the existing URL-encoded rows instead of replacing the body.
- `TextBody.tsx` keeps plain text copy/paste semantics and only changes the clipboard transport and error handling.

Clipboard failures and invalid paste content must use the notification adapter for user-visible feedback and may log contextual errors without logging clipboard contents.

### Spec-Specific Overrides

No overrides — see memory.

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

> If `.spec-lite/data_model.md` exists, reference it as the authoritative schema. Only list relevant tables and feature-specific extensions. If no data model is involved, write "N/A — no data model changes."

N/A — no persistent data model changes.

### Entities & Attributes

- **`IClipboardAdapter`**:
  - `readText(): Promise<Result<string, string>>` — reads plain text from the active platform clipboard.
  - `writeText(value: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>` — writes plain text to the active platform clipboard.
- **`ParsedClipboardEntry`**:
  - `key` (string) — parsed key from the clipboard payload.
  - `value` (string) — parsed value from the clipboard payload.
- **`RequestClipboardParseResult`**:
  - `entries` (`ParsedClipboardEntry[]`) — ordered parsed rows ready to append.
  - `format` (`'json-object' | 'equals-lines' | 'colon-lines' | 'urlencoded'`) — detected clipboard format for diagnostics and tests.

### Relationships

- `IPlatformAdapter` 1:1 `IClipboardAdapter` via a new `clipboard` property.
- Request editor components consume the clipboard adapter through a new `useClipboardAdapter()` convenience hook.

### Indexes & Constraints

- Parsing must preserve input order.
- Duplicate keys are valid and must not be deduplicated.
- Copy serialization excludes disabled rows and excludes incomplete rows.
- Paste append behavior must not remove or overwrite existing rows.

## 4. Files

List the files this feature creates or modifies:

- `packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts` — add clipboard adapter contract and wire it into `IPlatformAdapter`
- `packages/core/src/hooks/useAdapter.tsx` — add `useClipboardAdapter()` convenience hook
- `packages/core/src/utils/requestClipboard.ts` — shared request-editor clipboard parse/serialize helpers
- `packages/core/src/components/common/FormBody.tsx` — replace direct clipboard access, append parsed URL-encoded rows, keep existing styling
- `packages/core/src/components/common/RequestHeaders.tsx` — add copy/paste actions with shared request-editor styling and append behavior
- `packages/core/src/components/common/RequestParams.tsx` — add copy/paste actions with shared request-editor styling and append behavior
- `packages/core/src/components/common/TextBody.tsx` — route raw text clipboard behavior through the adapter and notifications
- `packages/core/src/test/utils/requestClipboard.test.ts` — utility tests for parser/serializer behavior
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/FormBody.test.tsx` — URL-encoded copy/paste behavior tests
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/RequestHeaders.test.tsx` — header copy/paste behavior tests
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/RequestParams.test.tsx` — param copy/paste behavior tests
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/TextBody.test.tsx` — raw body clipboard behavior tests
- `packages/core/src/test/mocks/mockAdapter.ts` — add clipboard adapter mocks and clipboard-state controls
- `packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts` — implement clipboard adapter using browser APIs
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts` — add clipboard request methods to the webview adapter bridge
- `packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts` — handle clipboard read/write requests in the extension host
- `packages/vscode/src/test/handlers/MessageHandler.test.ts` — VS Code clipboard bridge tests

## 5. Dependencies

Features, infrastructure, or libraries that must exist before this feature can be implemented:

- Existing platform adapter architecture in `packages/core`, `packages/web`, and `packages/vscode`
- Existing request-editor Zustand actions used by headers, params, raw body, and URL-encoded body

## 6. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Add platform clipboard adapter support

- [ ] **Implementation**: Extend `packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts` with `IClipboardAdapter`, add a `clipboard` property to `IPlatformAdapter`, add `useClipboardAdapter()` in `packages/core/src/hooks/useAdapter.tsx`, and update `packages/core/src/test/mocks/mockAdapter.ts` with configurable mock clipboard behavior. Implement the new adapter in `packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts`, and add a VS Code bridge path in `packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts` plus `packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts` for clipboard read/write requests.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Add adapter-level tests covering successful clipboard reads/writes and failure propagation through the mock adapter and VS Code message handler.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add JSDoc for `IClipboardAdapter`, the `clipboard` property on `IPlatformAdapter`, and the new `useClipboardAdapter()` hook.
- **Verify**: Web and VS Code adapters expose the same clipboard contract, and request-editor code no longer depends on platform globals for clipboard access.

### TASK-002: Build shared request-editor clipboard parsing and serialization

- [ ] **Implementation**: Create `packages/core/src/utils/requestClipboard.ts` with helpers that serialize enabled non-empty key/value rows to newline-delimited `key=value` text and parse clipboard input from JSON objects, URL-encoded text, newline-delimited `key=value`, and newline-delimited `key: value`. The parser must trim whitespace appropriately, ignore a trailing comma before a newline, preserve duplicates, preserve input order, and return a failure result when no valid entries are found.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Cover JSON object input, URL-encoded input, `key=value` lines, `key: value` lines, trailing commas, duplicate keys, disabled-row filtering during copy, incomplete-row exclusion during copy, and invalid payload rejection.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add JSDoc for public utility functions and brief inline comments explaining format detection precedence.
- **Verify**: The utility returns deterministic append-ready rows across all supported clipboard formats.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-003: Add copy/paste UX to request headers and params

- [ ] **Implementation**: Update `packages/core/src/components/common/RequestHeaders.tsx` and `packages/core/src/components/common/RequestParams.tsx` to add copy/paste buttons using the same compact action-button styling pattern already used in the request body editors. Copy must serialize only enabled non-empty rows. Paste must append parsed rows to the current list rather than replacing data, preserve duplicates, and maintain the editor’s trailing empty row behavior. Clipboard failures or invalid paste payloads must show user-facing notifications.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Add component tests confirming copy excludes disabled rows, paste appends instead of overwriting, duplicate keys remain present, and failure notifications are shown when clipboard reads fail or parsing yields no valid entries.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add component-level JSDoc or inline comments for the new clipboard action handlers.
- **Verify**: A user can copy existing headers/params, paste additional rows from any supported format, and keep the pre-existing rows intact.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002

### TASK-004: Fix URL-encoded body clipboard behavior in FormBody

- [ ] **Implementation**: Update `packages/core/src/components/common/FormBody.tsx` so copy uses the shared serializer and the clipboard adapter, paste uses the shared parser, and parsed rows are appended after existing URL-encoded rows instead of replacing the current body. Preserve the current button styling, current clear/add row behavior, and the existing dropdown/header layout.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Add component tests covering copy of enabled rows only, paste append behavior with pre-existing rows, JSON object paste, line-based paste, URL-encoded paste, duplicate keys, and user-facing notifications on clipboard or parse failure.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add concise inline comments around append logic and JSDoc for clipboard handlers if they are extracted.
- **Verify**: Pasting into URL-encoded body adds new rows to the end of the existing table and never clears previously entered rows.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002

### TASK-005: Move raw body clipboard behavior onto the adapter boundary

- [ ] **Implementation**: Update `packages/core/src/components/common/TextBody.tsx` so raw body copy/paste remains plain text but uses the new clipboard adapter and notification adapter. Do not change raw-body format detection, formatting, or quick-example behavior.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Add component tests confirming raw body copy writes the current text, paste replaces the raw body text with clipboard text, detected language updates still occur after paste, and clipboard failures show notifications.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update component JSDoc or inline comments to state that raw body clipboard access is platform-routed.
- **Verify**: Raw body UX remains unchanged to the user except that clipboard failures are handled consistently across platforms.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: Clipboard read/write failures return `Result` errors through the adapter and are surfaced via `notification.showNotification(...)`. Invalid paste content must show a non-destructive warning or info notification rather than silently failing.
- **Logging**: Platform implementations may log clipboard operation failures with operation context, but must not log clipboard contents.
- **Platform Independence**: `packages/core` must not call `navigator.clipboard`, `window`, `document.execCommand`, or VS Code APIs directly for this feature.
- **Performance**: Parsing only runs on explicit paste actions; no continuous parsing during typing.
